Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results state: 'AmOSC2 and AmOSC3 (Genbank Accession Nos. MT080938 and MT080939)'.

Source_Species.reason: AmOSC2 was cloned from Astragalus membranaceus throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently identifies the source plant as Astragalus membranaceus.

Applied_Species.reason: AmOSC2 was tested in A. membranaceus, Nicotiana benthamiana, and yeast strain GIL77; yeast is consistent with Saccharomyces cerevisiae as provided.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments used Astragalus membranaceus, Nicotiana benthamiana, and yeast GIL77.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Results and conclusion identify AmOSC2 as a β-amyrin synthase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The study concerns triterpenoid/saponin biosynthesis and identifies AmOSC2 as contributing to oleanane-type saponin biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: AmOSC2 is linked to β-amyrin and oleanane-type saponins including soyasaponin I, i.e. the oleanane-type/soyasaponin branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AmOSC2 cyclizes oxidosqualene to β-amyrin, creating the branch scaffold for downstream oleanane-type triterpenoids.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results show AmOSC2 converts 2,3-oxidosqualene to β-amyrin in microsomes and also produced minor dammarenediol-II in N. benthamiana and epoxydammara-3,25-diol in yeast.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Microsome assays explicitly used 2,3-oxidosqualene as substrate.

Primary_Product.reason: AmOSC2 yielded β-amyrin as the major product in N. benthamiana, yeast, and microsome assays.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper states AmOSC2 is associated with production of β-amyrin and soyasaponins/oleanane-type saponins in vivo.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is supported as a key scaffold/branch-defining enzyme and yield determinant, but the paper does not formally establish it as a control step beyond that wording.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: β-amyrin, soyasaponin I, astroolesaponin D, and robinioside B are all linked to AmOSC2 in the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The listed metabolites include both β-amyrin (a triterpene aglycone) and saponins; 'Triterpenoid saponin' does not fully cover all listed terminal metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: β-amyrin and downstream oleanane-type saponins showed higher content in underground parts/roots.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states triterpenoids/saponins have broad pharmacological activities and refers to medicinally important soyasaponins in Huang-Qi.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression increased β-amyrin, VIGS decreased β-amyrin, and expression correlated with higher oleanane-type saponin accumulation in roots.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported in Results: cloning/biochemical confirmation, higher underground expression, 65.7% increase on overexpression, 26.3% decrease by VIGS, positive correlations, and up to 12.8-fold increase with SIV→VFM mutant.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for AmOSC2 except RNAi did not yield a clean functional conclusion, but RNAi was indeed performed and discussed.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AmOSC2 is explicitly an oxidosqualene cyclase (OSC).

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microsomes were used for enzyme assays in yeast, but the paper does not directly report native subcellular localization of AmOSC2.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR showed much higher expression in underground than aerial parts.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: In A. membranaceus seedlings, AmOSC2 expression was strongly enriched in underground parts.

Omics_Data.reason: The Results list transcriptome mining from SRR923811, ERR706814, SRR5343992, and SRR5343993.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These growth conditions appear in Methods, and the statement cautiously says no special environmental factor was central; this is consistent with the paper.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper names vectors pEAQ-HT and pYES2, but does not explicitly name the promoter(s) in this text.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results state compensational increase of AmOSC3 expression was observed when AmOSC2 was interfered; mutagenesis also changed yield/by-product profile.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All points are supported: AmOSC2 has SIV instead of conserved VFM; I728F had strongest positive effect among single mutants; Y259A abolished activity; minor products were observed.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper concludes the motif offers opportunities for OSC engineering, and SIV→VFM markedly enhanced β-amyrin yield.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the paper.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results state AmOSC3 has Genbank accession MT080939.

Source_Species.reason: AmOSC3 was cloned from Astragalus membranaceus.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently identifies the source species as Astragalus membranaceus.

Applied_Species.reason: AmOSC3 was tested in A. membranaceus, Nicotiana benthamiana, and yeast GIL77.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments used Astragalus membranaceus, Nicotiana benthamiana, and yeast GIL77.

Enzyme_Name.reason: AmOSC3 is explicitly identified as a cycloartenol synthase.

EC_Number.reason: The paper does not provide an EC number.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: AmOSC3 is tied to cycloartane-type saponin/astragaloside biosynthesis and to cycloartenol as a plant sterol precursor.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and discussion link AmOSC3 to both cycloartane-type triterpenoid/astragaloside biosynthesis and sterol precursor formation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: AmOSC3 forms cycloartenol from oxidosqualene, an upstream scaffold-forming step.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Transient expression, yeast expression, and microsome assays all showed production of cycloartenol from 2,3-oxidosqualene.

Primary_Product.reason: Cycloartenol was the single major product of AmOSC3.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper states AmOSC3 is related to synthesis of astragalosides in roots and to cycloartenol as plant sterol precursor.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Supported as a key scaffold-forming enzyme, but not formally demonstrated to be rate-limiting.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Cycloartenol and astragalosides I-IV are all explicitly linked to AmOSC3 in Results/Discussion.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Cycloartenol is not a saponin, so 'Triterpenoid saponin' does not fully fit the whole listed terminal metabolite set.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Cycloartenol was detected in seeds and seedlings, while cycloartane-type saponins specifically existed in underground parts.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Discussion states astragalosides I-IV are major bioactive compounds with cardiovascular protective effects, and cycloartenol is a sterol precursor.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Overexpression increased cycloartenol, VIGS decreased cycloartenol, and transcript levels correlated with cycloartane-type saponins in roots.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is supported by Results: biochemical confirmation, broader expression with higher underground expression, 27.1% increase on overexpression, 61.0% decrease by VIGS, positive correlations, and reduced activity after VFN mutation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods except RNAi as a successful validation are supported; the paper did perform RNAi and reported problems specifically for AmOSC3 silencing.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AmOSC3 is explicitly an oxidosqualene cyclase.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Microsomes were prepared for assays, but the paper does not directly state subcellular localization of AmOSC3 in planta.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: AmOSC3 was expressed in all samples, with higher expression in underground parts.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The statement matches the seedling data: AmOSC3 in both aerial and underground parts; cycloartenol broad; astragalosides root-enriched.

Omics_Data.reason: The same four SRA datasets were mined to identify candidate OSC genes.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These growth conditions are described in Methods, and no special environmental treatment is central to interpretation.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Vectors pEAQ-HT and pYES2 are named, but promoter identities are not explicitly stated in the paper text provided.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results state silencing of sterol biosynthetic gene AmOSC3 was more likely to be detrimental, consistent with essential sterol role.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports that AmOSC3 contributes to both astragaloside-related specialized metabolism and core sterol metabolism, with downstream astragalosides restricted to underground tissues despite broader expression.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Engineering relevance is supported by the conclusion and the observed detrimental effects/dual role make the caution about fitness penalties a reasonable direct inference from Results and Discussion.

corrections.reason: The paper does not provide an EC number.
